title: TTRPG Librarian Bot — Internal Data Schemas (LLM Cache)
description: Formal JSON Schemas for the configuration, state structures, and on-the-wire payloads used by the Librarian Bot. Update atomically with any structural change.

Config.description: Subset of src/config.js runtime configuration. Every value here is the source of truth for that knob.

DM_ROLE_ID.description: Dungeon Master role — used by the slash-command permission check (interactions.js) and as one of the admin-tier quota checks.

ADMIN_ROLE_ID.description: Server Administrator role — independently grants the higher-tier DeepSeek quota (QUOTA_ADMIN_MAX_REQUESTS / QUOTA_ADMIN_WINDOW_HOURS) regardless of the slash-command permission rules.

TIMEZONE.description: IANA tz (e.g. Europe/Tallinn). Used by cron and the system help timestamp.

CRON_SCHEDULE_MONTHLY_MINI.description: Standard 5-field cron expression for the monthly mini-printing post.

DISCORD_FILE_LIMIT_DEFAULT.description: Default guild file upload limit (bytes); overridden by boost tier in getGuildFileLimit.

QUOTA_MAX_REQUESTS.description: Maximum DeepSeek-routed requests per user inside the sliding window.

QUOTA_WINDOW_HOURS.description: Sliding window length (hours) for the per-user quota.

QUOTA_STATE_PATH.description: Filesystem path of the persistent quota store (relative to WORKDIR).

SYSTEM_UPDATES_LIMIT.description: Number of git log entries shown in the system-updates thread. The actual git log call clamps to [1, 50] in getLastUpdates() for safety.

SYSTEM_UPDATES_STATE_PATH.description: Filesystem path of the system_state.json file (relative to WORKDIR). Stores { systemUpdatesThreadId, systemUpdatesMessageId }.

SYSTEM_UPDATES_THREAD_NAME.description: Display name of the auto-created updates thread. The 📜 prefix is cosmetic only.

IGPU_RENDER_NODE.description: Path to the VAAPI render node inside the container (default /dev/dri/renderD128). When this file is absent the local iGPU stage is skipped.

IGPU_VIDEO_BITRATE_MULTIPLIERS.description: Bitrate multipliers (as a fraction of the size budget) tried in order by every VAAPI encode stage.

IGPU_MAX_VIDEO_BITRATE.description: Upper clamp for the calculated video bitrate (bits per second) used by the iGPU and network VAAPI encoders.

IGPU_MIN_VIDEO_BITRATE.description: Lower clamp for the calculated video bitrate (bits per second) used by the iGPU and network VAAPI encoders.

QuotaState.description: Persistent per-user quota tracker stored at QUOTA_STATE_PATH. Each key is a Discord user snowflake; each value is a { regular, admin } bucket pair. The two buckets are tracked independently so a regular session never decrements the admin quota and vice versa. Legacy single-array entries (where the value is a flat array of timestamps, from before the admin tier was added) are read once and migrated to the bucket shape on load.

additionalProperties.description: Per-user bucket pair. A legacy flat array is also accepted on read and treated as the 'regular' bucket.

regular.description: Epoch ms timestamps consumed against the regular tier within the rolling window.

admin.description: Epoch ms timestamps consumed against the admin tier within the rolling window.

oneOf.title: LegacyFlatArray
oneOf.description: Pre-admin-tier format: treated as the 'regular' bucket on read.

QuotaProfile.description: Per-user quota tier. 'regular' consumes from the QUOTA_MAX_REQUESTS / QUOTA_WINDOW_HOURS bucket; 'admin' consumes from the QUOTA_ADMIN_MAX_REQUESTS / QUOTA_ADMIN_WINDOW_HOURS bucket. Tracked independently per user.

QuotaDecision.description: Result of consumeQuota(userId, isAdmin) — the runtime decision passed back to handleRagQuery.

allowed.description: true if the request fits inside the user's window for the selected profile and a slot was consumed.

used.description: Slots consumed inside the current window for the selected profile AFTER this decision.

remaining.description: Slots still available in the current window for the selected profile AFTER this decision.

resetAt.description: Unix epoch milliseconds when the oldest slot in the selected profile's bucket expires (null when no slots are used).

IgpuDetection.description: Result of hasSupportedIgpu() — whether the host exposes a usable Intel iGPU render node for VAAPI transcoding.

supported.description: true only when the CPU model is in the supported list (currently Intel N100 / N150) AND the render node exists in the container.

model.description: Contents of the first `model name` field from /proc/cpuinfo (empty string when cpuinfo is unreadable).

renderNode.description: Path to the VAAPI render node used by the iGPU stage.

reason.description: Human-readable explanation of the decision, suitable for log lines.

ChannelMetadata.description: Metadata token embedded in a campaign channel's topic. Two formats: SETUP (pre-OP) and LIBRARIAN_DATA (post-OP). getLibrarianData() in src/utils/helpers.js only recognises LIBRARIAN_DATA; the SETUP regex is duplicated in messageCreate.js and interactions.js for pre-OP permission checks.

oneOf.title: SetupTopic
oneOf.description: Pre-OP format written by /new-campaign and /new-private-campaign. Topic starts with 'SETUP|'.

users.description: Snowflakes to auto-assign the campaign role to when the OP is posted.

oneOf.title: ActiveTopic
oneOf.description: Post-OP format written by the OP workflow. Topic ends with ' [LIBRARIAN_DATA|DM:<id>|ROLE:<id>]'.

VideoStage.description: Stage label carried in FfmpegProgress objects. Mapped to user-facing strings ('local iGPU' / 'NAS iGPU' / 'local CPU') in src/services/instagram.js.

FfmpegProgress.description: Progress callback payload emitted by runCommandWithProgress each time ffmpeg prints a `time=HH:MM:SS.cs` line to stderr.

percent.description: 0-100; computed from the latest ffmpeg time and the input's total duration.

TrackedMessage.description: In-memory association of a bot- or webhook-sent message id with the user who triggered it (used by /roll, !pin, etc.). Capped at 1000 entries by messageTracker.js.

timestamp.description: Unix epoch milliseconds when the association was recorded.

LastUpdatesEntry.description: One line of the system-help message. Format produced by getLastUpdates() in src/utils/helpers.js: `- YYYY-MM-DD: <subject> ([<shortHash>](<commitUrl>))`.

LibrarianQuirk.description: One of the 10 LIBRARIAN_QUIRKS in src/services/rag.js — a 1-sentence in-character action that flavours the system prompt. Selected per query by seed % quirks.length.

FallbackRoast.description: One of the FALLBACK_ROASTS in src/config.js — used by /roll when the Ollama roast generator is offline. Wrapped in italics with an '(AI backend offline, using archived roast)' tail by the handler.

SystemState.description: Persistent state at SYSTEM_UPDATES_STATE_PATH. Used by index-librarian.js's ClientReady handler to find the existing system-updates thread + the bot's first message inside it, edit in place, and re-apply the lock. If the stored ids reference a missing thread/message, the handler self-heals by creating a fresh thread+message and overwriting the state. Both ids are nullable to allow partial state on first run.

systemUpdatesThreadId.description: Id of the auto-created 📜 Updates Log thread. Null on first run before the thread is created.

systemUpdatesMessageId.description: Id of the bot's first non-OP message inside the thread (the one holding the rolling 'Last N Updates' list). Edited in place on every restart.

CompressResult.description: Return value of compressVideoToFit on success. Buffer is the .mp4 (or .ts if remux failed) bytes; ext is the file extension Discord should use.
